name: Web Routing
description: Standard that owns the routing domain — a declarable route-format contract, the URL-as-state seam many stateful components share, technical routing configuration, and sitemap derivation, homing what is today scattered across Navigation (the UX axes), the router block (the imperative runtime), and Web Guards (route guards). Routes are authored as declarative DOM — <template route="/users/:id"> children of <we-route-view>, with URLPattern (Baseline 2025) as the pattern grammar — and webrouting derives a serializable route-map projection for the non-DOM consumers (sitemap, prerender, config tooling) so the route table reads without instantiating the running app. The URL is treated as a state store many components read and write, with a per-slice persistence dimension (url | session | memory, generalized from the Navigation Intent) and a permissive default — nothing is forced. Per the constellation rule the contract lives here in @webeverything and the runtime ships in Frontier UI. Shape ratified in #1685 and scaffolded under epic #1684, mirroring how Web Graph was stood up.
summary: Owns the routing domain — declarative routes, URL-as-state, and sitemap derivation.
